text: The surgeon isolates the middle lobe of the lung, separating it from the adjacent upper and lower lobes, preparing for resection.
The surgeon opens the anterior mediastinal pleura and begins dissection of the superior pulmonary vein, exposing the vein's structure within the surgical field.
The surgeon isolates the middle lobe vein, carefully separating it from surrounding structures within the thoracic cavity.
The surgeon performs dissection of the fissure between the pulmonary lobes, carefully separating the tissue planes to facilitate lobectomy.
The surgeon carefully dissects the lymph node that is compressing the middle lobe bronchus, revealing its position relative to the bronchial structure.
The surgeon performs dissection of the middle lobe bronchus, carefully separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon uses conventional Watts instruments to encircle the middle lobe bronchus, preparing it for further manipulation or division.
The surgeon performs dissection of the middle lobe artery, carefully isolating it from surrounding tissues within the surgical field.
The surgeon encircles the middle lobe artery and places hemoclips on it, securing the vessel within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses scissors to divide the middle lobe artery, completing the separation of this vessel from surrounding structures.
